changes_made: Changed k8s_service_mesh_dos_protection_enabled to k8s_service_dos_protection_enabled for consistency with resource naming conventions
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes features that can help mitigate denial-of-service attacks. However, the naming for the service mesh function was adjusted to align with the existing naming conventions. The use of NetworkPolicies, API server rate limiting, service mesh capabilities, and cluster autoscaling are appropriate strategies for addressing SC-5(2) in a Kubernetes context.
naming_improvements: Use 'service' instead of 'service_mesh' for resource name to maintain consistency with naming conventions
title: Denial-Of-Service Protection - Denial - of - service protection | capacity , bandwidth , and redundancy manage
step1_reasoning: The NIST SC-5(2) control focuses on denial-of-service protection through capacity, bandwidth, and redundancy management. In Kubernetes, this can be addressed by implementing NetworkPolicies to limit traffic, configuring API server rate limiting to prevent abuse, leveraging service mesh capabilities for traffic management and resilience, and using cluster autoscaling to manage resource capacity and redundancy.
